silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: Start from the bronze qualys_vulnerabilities_api_2_0_fo_qid_search_list_dynamic_dynamic_list ingest table as-is
Filter to records representing discovered vulnerability findings (exclude scan metadata rows that are not vulnerability instances)
Normalize and deduplicate vulnerabilities so there is at most one row per (asset, cve_id, severity) per day
Derive a snapshot_date from the ingestion timestamp truncated to day and include a discovery_date and last_seen_date where available

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_vulnerability_count_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Aggregate daily silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily to weekly counts for reporting and trend comparison

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: Start from the bronze qualys_vulnerabilities_api_2_0_fo_qid_search_list_dynamic_dynamic_list ingest table
Filter to active vulnerability findings and map severity taxonomy into standardized tiers (CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW) if needed
Produce one row per (snapshot_date, severity, asset) by deduplicating repeated findings on the same asset within a day
Add snapshot_date (day) and a severity_normalized column

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_vulnerability_count_by_severity_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Aggregate daily severity counts to weekly grain for dashboards

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: Use the silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily table (derived from the bronze table) which has one row per vulnerability per asset per day
Filter to records where severity_normalized = CRITICAL and state indicates not remediated or not fixed
Aggregate by snapshot_date to compute daily counts of CRITICAL vulnerabilities
Populate a 30-day rolling window column to support trend visualizations

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_critical_vulnerability_trend_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Roll up daily critical counts to weekly to smooth short-term fluctuations

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: Start from the silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily table
Filter to vulnerabilities where the finding state indicates 'UNPATCHED', 'FIX_AVAILABLE' or other non-remediated states
Group by snapshot_date to get daily counts of unpatched vulnerabilities
Flag assets with repeated unpatched critical/high findings to enable follow-up and aging calculations

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_unpatched_vulnerabilities_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Aggregate unpatched counts from silver daily to weekly for SLA/threshold monitoring

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: Use silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily containing discovery_date and snapshot_date
For each snapshot_date, count vulnerabilities whose discovery_date is equal to the snapshot_date (new discoveries)
Compute a daily time series of new discovery counts and a 7-day moving average to show discovery rate trends
Flag spikes by comparing current daily discovery to recent baseline

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_new_vulnerability_discovery_rate_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Provide weekly summaries and trend deltas versus prior weeks

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: Start from silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily which contains discovery_date and last_seen_date
For active/unremediated vulnerabilities, compute age as snapshot_date minus discovery_date
Aggregate ages by snapshot_date to calculate median and mean age per day
Produce a daily time series of median age to show whether remediation velocity is improving

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_vulnerability_age_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Weekly medians smooth daily volatility for SLA reporting

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: Use silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily which has asset identifiers and snapshot_date
Group rows by asset_id (or asset name) and snapshot_date and count distinct cve_id per asset
Produce a daily time series per asset and optionally rank assets by vulnerability count
Expose filters to limit to severity tiers or business-critical asset tags

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_vulnerability_count_by_asset_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Weekly rollups for asset-level dashboards and remediation prioritization

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: Start from silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily which includes cve_id, severity and asset identifiers
For each snapshot_date, count number of unique assets affected by each cve_id
Produce a daily ranked list of top N CVEs by exposed asset count
Track the exposure trend of each top CVE over time to detect widening exposure

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_top_cve_exposure_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Weekly top-CVE exposure tables for executive reporting and patch campaigns

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: From the bronze ingest, identify distinct assets observed by Qualys per snapshot_date
Define the expected asset population if available, otherwise use an inferred baseline of known assets from the same table over a 30-day window
Compute daily scan coverage as (distinct assets scanned on snapshot_date) divided by (expected distinct assets) and produce a time series
Flag days with coverage below target thresholds for follow-up

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_scan_coverage_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Weekly scan coverage rates for compliance and SOC2 evidence

silver_table_suggestion.name: silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily
calculation_steps: Use the bronze table to identify vulnerabilities that report fix availability or patch status fields and join to silver_qualys_vulnerabilities_daily records by asset and cve_id
Classify findings into 'compliant' (patched/fixed) vs 'non-compliant' (unpatched/patch available) using available state fields
Aggregate by snapshot_date to compute a daily patch compliance proxy percentage (patched / total applicable findings)
Note: this is a proxy; full patch compliance requires dedicated patch-management data

gold_table_suggestion.name: gold_patch_compliance_proxy_weekly
gold_table_suggestion.note: Weekly proxy rates for showing patching progress; integrate patch management system to replace proxy
